summary: Targeted retest of iteration_7 HIGH bug: mobile bottom-nav badge overlap at 390x844. All 5 mobile nav items (Home, Ordini, Clienti, Catalogo, Setup) are now clickable with a normal (non-forced) click and each navigates to its correct route. Fix in AppShell.js (pt-1.5 pb-14, z-[60], main pb-32) confirmed working.
critical_code_review_comments: AppShell.js mobile <nav> bottom bar (y=743-788, height=45) now correctly sits above the fixed 'Made with Emergent' badge (y=788-828). Zero overlap in the vertical axis; badge no longer intercepts pointer events on any of the 5 nav items.
test_credentials: Pilot mode auto-login worked, no explicit credentials needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: iteration_7 HIGH bug (mobile bottom-nav badge overlap) is fully fixed. All 5 mobile nav testids (nav-dashboard-mobile, nav-orders-mobile, nav-customers-mobile, nav-catalog-mobile, nav-setup-mobile) navigate correctly at 390x844 with normal clicks (no force). Badge sits below the nav bar. Desktop flows were already 100% in iteration_7 and were intentionally not re-tested per request.
rca of the issue: Root cause was the fixed 'Made with Emergent' badge (bottom-right, ~z-index high) intercepting pointer events on the two right-most nav items (Catalogo, Setup) which sat at the very bottom edge. Fix lifts the entire mobile nav upward via bottom-0 + pb-14 padding (making the nav's clickable area end at y=788, exactly where the badge starts) and sets z-[60] so the nav is above the badge stacking context. Main content compensates with pb-32 to avoid content being hidden. Verified via measured bounding boxes and successful non-force clicks on all 5 items.
